prompt: The array fill loop does not initialize all elements. What is the bug?
choices: new int[5] does not allocate memory for all 5 elements
The loop starts at i=1 skipping arr[0]; arr[0] remains the default value of 0
i * 10 causes integer overflow for large arrays
arr.length should be arr.length - 1 in the condition
explanation: The loop initializes arr[1] through arr[4] but skips arr[0]. arr[0] remains 0 (the default for int arrays). Fix: start at i=0: for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++).
